skill.name: google-agents-cli-scaffold
skill.description: Scaffold new Google ADK agent projects or enhance existing ones with deployment, CI/CD, and infrastructure. Covers scaffold create, enhance, and upgrade commands, template options, and the prototype-first workflow.
skill.summary: Create and enhance Google ADK agent projects with deployment, CI/CD, and infrastructure scaffolding using the agents-cli tool.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation guide for Google's ADK (Agent Development Kit) CLI scaffolding tool. Static analysis produced 223+ findings, all confirmed false positives after manual review. The 'backtick execution' detections (206) are Markdown inline code formatting for CLI flag names like --agent, --prototype, not actual code execution. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives from pattern matching on words like 'adk' and 'Agent'. The single 'Hardcoded URL' is a legitimate documentation link to the uv package manager installation guide. 'Temp directory access' and 'Environment file access' are documentation describing recommended workflows. No executable code, scripts, or malicious intent was found in the skill content.

low_findings.title: Documentation references external commands
low_findings.description: The skill documentation shows CLI commands (agents-cli scaffold create, enhance, upgrade) that the user would run. These are documentation examples, not code executed by the skill itself. The skill is a guide, not a script.

content.user_title: Scaffold Google ADK Agent Projects with One Command
content.value_statement: Starting a new AI agent project requires setting up deployment configs, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure files. This skill guides Claude through using the agents-cli to scaffold complete Google ADK projects with the right architecture choices, avoiding common mistakes like directory conflicts and wrong CI/CD runners.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through creating new Google ADK agent projects using agents-cli scaffold create
Enhance existing projects with deployment targets (agent_runtime, cloud_run, GKE) via scaffold enhance
Upgrade existing projects to newer agents-cli versions with scaffold upgrade
Map user terminology (Vertex AI, Agent Engine) to current CLI flag values
Recommend the prototype-first workflow: start simple, add deployment later
limitations: Requires agents-cli to be installed (uv tool install google-agents-cli)
Does not write or modify agent code itself (use google-agents-cli-adk-code for that)
Does not handle deployment operations directly (use google-agents-cli-deploy)
Project names limited to 26 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only

use_cases.title: New agent project setup
use_cases.description: Developer wants to start a new AI agent project with Google ADK and needs help choosing the right template, deployment target, and CI/CD runner.

use_cases.title: Adding deployment to prototype
use_cases.description: Developer has a working agent prototype and needs to add production deployment, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure configs.

use_cases.title: Project upgrade assistance
use_cases.description: Developer has an existing ADK project and wants to upgrade to the latest agents-cli version while preserving customizations.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new prototype agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Google ADK agent project called 'my-chatbot' using the standard adk template. I want to start with a prototype to iterate quickly before adding deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner starting their first agent project

prompt_templates.title: Add deployment to existing project
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an existing ADK project in the current directory. Add Cloud Run deployment and GitHub Actions CI/CD to it. The agent code is in the app/ directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer ready to move from prototype to production

prompt_templates.title: Create RAG agent with vector search
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a RAG agent that uses Vertex AI Vector Search for document retrieval. Deploy it to Agent Runtime. The project should be called 'doc-search-agent'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building a retrieval-augmented generation system

prompt_templates.title: Create A2A agent project
prompt_templates.prompt: I need an A2A (agent-to-agent) protocol project using Google ADK. Scaffold it with Cloud Run deployment and use --agent adk_a2a template.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building multi-agent coordination system

output_examples.input: User: Create a new ADK agent project called 'support-bot' with prototype mode
output: - What will the agent do? (customer support, FAQ answering, ticket routing?)
- Do you want a prototype first or full deployment?
For a prototype to iterate quickly, I'll run:

output_examples.input: User: Add deployment to my existing ADK project
output: I'll help you add deployment. First, a few questions:
For Agent Runtime with GitHub Actions:

output_examples.input: User: What's the difference between 'agentic_rag' and 'adk' templates?
output: - adk: Standard ADK agent (default). Good for most use cases.
- adk_a2a: Agent-to-agent coordination using A2A protocol.
- agentic_rag: RAG with data ingestion pipeline. Requires provisioning a datastore before testing.
For a simple chatbot, use 'adk'. For document Q&A, use 'agentic_rag' with --datastore agent_platform_vector_search.

best_practices: Always load google-agents-cli-workflow Phase 0 and clarify requirements before running scaffold create
Start with --prototype flag to skip CI/CD and Terraform for faster iteration, add deployment later with scaffold enhance
Pass all required params explicitly as CLI flags (strict programmatic mode) to avoid UsageError
Use --dry-run with scaffold upgrade to preview changes before applying them
Auto-detect the guidance filename based on your IDE (GEMINI.md, CLAUDE.md, or AGENTS.md)
anti_patterns: Do NOT mkdir the project directory before running scaffold create, the CLI creates it automatically and pre-creating causes errors
Do NOT change the model in existing code unless explicitly asked by the user
Do NOT write A2A code from scratch, the API surface changes across versions, always use --agent adk_a2a to scaffold
Do NOT create a Git repo or push to remote without asking the user first

faq.question: What is the agents-cli scaffold tool?
faq.answer: The agents-cli scaffold tool is a command-line utility from Google that creates and enhances Google ADK (Agent Development Kit) agent projects. It generates deployment configs, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure files automatically.

faq.question: How do I install agents-cli?
faq.answer: Install it using uv: 'uv tool install google-agents-cli'. You need to install uv first from docs.astral.sh/uv if you don't have it. The skill requires this tool to function.

faq.question: What is the prototype-first workflow?
faq.answer: Start by creating a project with the --prototype flag, which skips CI/CD and Terraform setup. Focus on getting the agent working first. Later, use scaffold enhance to add deployment, CI/CD, and infrastructure when ready for production.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for deployment operations?
faq.answer: No, this skill only covers scaffolding (create, enhance, upgrade). For deployment operations, use the google-agents-cli-deploy skill instead. This skill is specifically for project setup and enhancement.

faq.question: What deployment targets are supported?
faq.answer: Three targets: agent_runtime (managed by Google, sessions handled automatically), cloud_run (container-based, requires Dockerfile), and gke (GKE Autopilot with full Kubernetes control). You can also use 'none' for code-only projects.

faq.question: What are the project name requirements?
faq.answer: Project names must be 26 characters or less, using only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. The CLI creates the directory automatically, so do not run mkdir before scaffold create.
